reviews.review_description: I all most couldnt find this game cause i was like what the heii is tale from the zone. That wasnt it original title now havent play this in like two years. But it was a pretty awesome game so here i got looking for it. And see tale from the zone im like WTF is that. I didnt by that. The twilight zone label is what made me buy it that all im say why the name change

Good game but when the graphics were updated the flying monsters from story two were gone. Please 
bring them back. Otherwise it is a great game and really feels like being in the twilight zone! Also why the name change?

reviews.review_title: Can you get a new game on oculus quest 3
reviews.review_description:
😍😉🥲🙃😍😗🥲😝😝😝vdftxs

vr

This is an awesome punch of nostalgia as I watched the show as a kid. Experiencing it in VR is a dream! Feels like a bargain getting basically three different types of games in one! Loving the constant feeling of thread and the non stop action. Being chased by a murderous NPC to squishing monsters by dropping junk cars on top of them! If you’re a thrill chaser, this game is for you! Btw, ignore the useless negative reviews. They clearly failed at escaping from the NPC in chapter 1. Just because you fail at a game doesn’t mean it’s bad.

The Twilight Zone VR successfully captures the strange and unsettling spirit of the series: from the very first chapter, the atmosphere is heavy, the tension palpable, and the jump scares work very well.
The sets, the sound effects, the shadows—everything contributes to creating a subtle unease that doesn't easily dissipate.
Each episode offers slightly different gameplay: stealth and paranoia in a building, post-apocalyptic exploration and survival in a devastated landscape, escape aboard an alien spaceship…
This variety avoids redundancy and maintains suspense with each new entry.
You never know what awaits you or how you'll get out alive.
If you enjoy psychological horror, a mysterious atmosphere, and unexpected narratives, The Twilight Zone VR is worth checking out.
It won't keep you on the edge of your seat for hours, but each chapter is a gateway to the unknown and an excellent reminder that VR can pack a punch when it dares to venture into the bizarre!

reviews.review_title: dududu and I'm in the twilight zone
reviews.review_description:
Not finished the third chapter yet and also haven't played the new visuals. I did however really enjoy the first 2 chapters. Was the AI in chapter one frustrating? Yes, but how fulfilling it felt being able to beat it in the end was good! I enjoyed the 2nd chapter as a shooter with a little bit of a story in there. Got me a few good jumpscares though! Can't wait to finish it entirely with the third chapter and the new visual update!

Would be great if additional chapters could get added eventually or maybe a cheap DLC of the sort to add to it all. However the price for basically 3 games/ long chapters is still worth it in my opinion.

Stupid game.
You go through  a building and a guy is always chasing you.
He finds you where ever you are. You can't even hide under a table.
I had to repeat some parts of the game again and again and again and again....
It got headache.
There is no option to change the level of difficulty.
So for me it is a waste of money.
Awesome game fun train
Such good fun. Particularly if you enjoy the TV shows. Must buy!
Ones i started playing it it felt so real
Love the game! Great experience

Ok so I jump within 2 minutes of playing, I was not expecting that.
I watched this a young teen fixated on sci fi, and thisjust captures everything about the show, with the iconic theme tune and narration.
 
TTZ is pretty intense but not super scary, 
Just started so I will add more as I go deeper, but hiding is what I do best in this type of game waiting for the next jump scare.
Well done devs it brings back great memories.

So far, so good! Not a lot of details to get you started but doesn't take long to figure it out. Love the titles sequence. I've only played a few hours but have been successfully moving through. Basically I'm avoiding a come-to-life video character while moving through the office building. Jump scares definitely possible! Not too challenging but fun!!

This is a spooky, scary one!

Step into the…. Twilight Zone!! 

Within minutes I was getting a jump scare, and I don’t jump easily lol

Very cautious, atmospheric game play… spooky armadillo!!

I like the presentation and graphic designs.

Fear of not knowing what’s going to come next, like the famous TV series. The sounds play a big part in the game too, playing on your mind.

One for the spooky game players
First time playing and it is truly realistic to The Twilight Zone genre in suspense and style.

I nearly didn’t buy this, after reading some of the negative reviews. I’m glad I did, though, because I am thoroughly enjoying it.

I have been a Twilight Zone fan since I first saw it in the 1960s (yes, I’m that old). Although the 3 new stories have been modernised, to me they retain some of the weird flavour and occasional sense of humour of the old TV show.

Once I got the hang of the controls, I had a great time exploring everything in each room. In particular, in chapter 2, spending some time checking all the papers, bottles and sundry items allowed me to immerse myself in the world before starting the game proper.

If you enjoyed the original series of Twilight Zone, or you like your games atmospheric and thoughtful, but not too difficult, you might enjoy this. I certainly did. Money well spent, in my opinion. Well done, the developers.

I love the “The Twilight Zone” franchise so I was very excited to finally get this on sale. I’ve been able to play for maybe 15-20 minutes and had to put the game down to go run an errand. Upon my return and after relaunching the game, I get a message telling me that I’ve unlocked one of the extra visual modes. I went to check it out, but it crashed the game. Tried to restart the game multiple times but it would get through the opening skippable scenes before instantly crashing as soon as it would have to load anything else. I tried restarting the system and got the same results. I then tried a fresh reinstall of the game; it worked, but loaded in with the extra/alt. visuals turned on (unlike last time where it would just crash). The game worked fine up until I went into the settings to reset the visuals to its original settings, and I’m once again having the exact same issue(s) as before. Going to have to resort to ANOTHER fresh reinstall and completely avoiding the graphical options like the plague.

Broken mess…
Great game for anyone who has seen the show before. Nostalgic, scary and full of adventure. This is one of my favorite titles from FunTrain.
It was tedious and boring.

I don’t know what it is with franchise VR games but they never actually capture the essence of the franchise they’re supposed to be portraying. This one for example. The first “episode” is… eh… something. It’s honestly the best one in the game because it’s actually about a bad person who get their comeuppance. But the gameplay is annoying and repetitive and completely lacks any heart. It’s just a basic ai chasing you around a musty building for 30 minutes.

The second and third episodes can be summed up by “there was a normal person…and then something WEIRD happened…and they did something about it…”

The 2nd episode is just a crappy shooter with an even crappier storyline. They were trying for psychological horror but it quickly devolves into the cheesiest thing I’ve ever seen.

The 3rd episode is just “Into The Radius” if it was made by someone hitting a computer with a wrench and calling it a game.

It’s barely fun to play so if it ever goes on sale for less than $10 it’s worth it, but otherwise PLEASE don’t waste your money.

Like many, I'm a huge fan of The Twilight Zone, however this game is nothing like it. The only resemblances are the use of its name and theme in the introduction, along with using Rod Serling's opening monologue.

Outside of that, this game is a really lazy set of 3 survival horror games (really bad ones). The first chapter is like a terrible browser game version of Alien Isolation but with terrible AI and annoying respawn points for the enemy. It uses a gimmick of using your own voice to taunt you but it gets old instantly. The 2nd chapter is a bad shooter where you use something like a potato gun that's extremely tedious to play and you die and start over if you miss. Didn't even bother with the other 3rd but reviews show or say it's more of the same lazy shooting and stealth mechanics.

The controls and options are also terrible in this game. You walk insanely slow and can't change your walking speed in the settings. You can sprint but only for a short period of time before running out of breath. This makes this game even more tedious to get through. Also no other gameplay options so this bad gameplay can't even be made easier to get through the story. Expect slogging through miserable gameplay just to experience the story. 

The stories themselves are also trite and completely out of touch with the show and anything Rod Serling wrote. There's nothing "mind-bending" about any of it, it's typical tropes like alien abductions, post apocalypse, lawnmower man nonsense. The Twilight Zone is not generic horror (which is what this game relies on) and the game should've been more of an interactive episode of the show, not lazy stealth and shooting mechanics.

This is an easy skip and sadly wasted opportunity for an IP that has a lot of potential for VR experiences.

The only thing I will say about this game is that it sucks I thought I was going to get a scary game but the enemy’s are really bad and aren’t scary and it’s really boring after a couple of minutes and half the time I don’t know where to go it’s just walking around half of the game but the story’s are okay and I’m just disappointed so this game is dookie

developer_response.body: Haha, we appreciate your honesty! We're sorry to hear that our game didn't meet your expectations. We understand that not every game is for everyone, and we appreciate your excrement-based feedback nonetheless.

reviews.review_description: Message me for 25% off

(Original review below)

Now that the sound issues have been resolved, and subtitles have been added, this is just about everything I could have wanted. 




The atmosphere and writing, even if a little hokey at times, is everything I wanted from a “Twilight Zone” game. It’s really quite frightening at times; however, as scary as the writing may be, it has nothing on the horrific sound design.

Sound effects blare as voices turn into but a whisper. Try as you might you’ll never find volume adjustment, as they don’t exist in this menu. You’re last hope is to put subtitles on… yet they are nowhere to be found. See this isn’t you’re average run of the mill options menu, this is “The Twilight Zone”.

If the sound issues are resolved this would definitely be a 5 star game!

developer_response.body: Josh, we listened!  Thank you so much for your kind words about the game.  We hope to bring you more Twilight Zone episodes in the near future.

reviews.review_description:
Help I'm stepping into the Twilight Zone:
Twilight Zone VR has three unique episodes. Each episode is its own story in a shared universe. Each episode has a distinct style to it that sometimes feels like three different games, but in the best possible way. There’s closets to hide in, puzzles to solve, monsters to shoot, and mysteries to uncover.

The place is a madhouse. Feels like being cloned:
There's subtle references that enhance the experience if you're familiar with the original series, films, or modern remakes; but this game's stories are not connected to the TV/film universe so they play perfectly independently. 

My beacon's been moved under moon and star:
Each episode can be completed in less than an hour, but I highly recommend taking your time. There's plenty of areas that aren't on the main story's path; and while the linear story isn't exactly open-world, exploring the secret rooms and reading in-universe newspapers enrich the experience.

Where am I to go, now that I've gone too far?
Twilight Zone is a fantastic bargain if you're a fan of puzzle/thriller/horror games! The episodic format in the shared-universe is ripe for expansion, and I hope DLC episodes are on the horizon.
Corny….  Waste of money

I had my son try it.   When I came down the oculus was on the floor and he was standing away from it.    It is intense for sure.  I’ve now Almost finished the first story and am brave enough to now go back to it.  After the first try I put the oculus away for more than 2 weeks before trying again.    I’d recommend it.  It will be the first game I have my friends try going forward I think.

So good! Only got maybe 3 hours play, taking my time, but it was VERY hard to put down. Loved the use of voice in the 1st chapter, especially as an actor/voice actor I had a lot of fun with it. I loved all of these and I want more. 3rd one was probably my favourite. Will happily pay for DLC. Only request would be an ability to skip the intro after watching it the first time, but the intro is pretty cool so it doesn't lose a star for that.

developer_response.body: Hi Robin, thank you for saying such positive things.  Regarding the ability to skip the intro,  simply hold down A when it starts- you'll proceed right to the menu!

reviews.review_title: Eh, just wanted so much more
reviews.review_description:
Coming from someone who absolutely loves the Twilight Zone IP and waited for months to play this experience, it’s just… Eh. It’s fine. And if I’m going to be blunt, it’s forgettable in its current state. 

Everything feels rushed and that’s evident after the start up screen, which gives the player the false impression of immersion “into the Twilight Zone” via the intro. It’s arguably the most well made part of the game. 

The first episode has a fun gimmick which I won’t spoil but if you’ve played Alien Isolation, this will feel like a MAJOR downgrade because it is. The stalker character is laughable and not in the least bit intimidating. Although I may sound harsh, it was still fun running and hiding around, trying not to be seen. 

The second is HORRIFIC. Period. And that’s not a compliment. Has no roots to the brand and feels SO laborious to play. You’re on rails the whole time and there’s virtually nothing to see or pick up around you besides a giant sun and newspapers. You’ll find “guns”(?) but in reality they’re steampunk renditions of pea-shooters with zero weight and punch. Oh, and also… the monsters are… Crab spiders and little goblins. “Cool” said no one. The story here makes no sense and I couldn’t wait to start the next episode, just to get out of this one. Overall, the concept was cool but convoluted and poorly executed, plus the lack of proper funding for such a blockbuster idea kept it shackled to the ground and feeling bland. The only saving grace about this episode were the whispers you’ll randomly hear, making you second guess everything around you. That’s actually cool.

The third episode actually gave me hope and kept me from thinking this game was a waste of time. Totally on brand for the IP and the twist felt more grounded despite the second half turning into more of the same from episode 2 (shoot brainless monsters, rinse, repeat). Gun play is still very wonky. I almost wish they scrapped the gun mechanic completely in favor of better monster designs, more interactivity w/environments, better level design… I dunno… there’s just so much more to be desired and SO MUCH CONTENT TO PULL FROM IF YOU’RE USING THE TWILIGHT ZONE IP.

There’s a certain level of camp & creepiness that I feel the devs nailed here despite some glaring issues like the graphics, lack of scares and interaction, “guns”, not using Serling’s voice/likeness, and the entirety of episode 2.

Hopefully there’s more to come because I’d love to see a redemption story - VR renditions of episodes like “nightmare at 30,000 ft” (the gorilla monster on the side of the wing) would be amazing. SCARIER or more inspired episodes and a graphics overhaul would elevate the game to a 4-5 star experience.

I really wanted to like it. I like the twilight zone shows.  But after reading all the reviews that hyped it up, it's just not what I was expecting it to be. It seems like a missed opportunity. But I paid for it so I'm stuck with it.  I will definitely start looking more into youtube videos on games in the future.

I bought this game the day it came out and experienced no bugs/glitches. Each chapter was engaging and unique, and the stories were fire. The replay ability isn’t as bad as the other reviews are saying. The game offers many achievements and, once achieved, rewards you with different art styles to play with. I’m praying Fun Train adds some DLC’s because this game exceeded my expectations.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for such kind words- everybody involved worked extremely hard on this and it's nice to see that it's appreciated. There are many addiitonal stories we would like to tell.

reviews.review_description:
I genuinely didn't know what to expect going into this game, but it did not disappoint! From the very first second the game is loaded it intends on keeping you on tenterhooks and does not intend on letting go! 

The intro was amazing to go through in vr and brought back memories of The Twilight Zone show! Really enjoyed the game and can't wait to throw some friends in there too!
Very fun definitely worth the wait. Just wish there were more chapters but hopefully more will be added in later updates. definitely think fun train nails horror.

developer_response.body: Don't forget Pocket Money Games!  We have some surprises in store for Twilight Zone - stay tuned and thanks for the positive comments.

reviews.review_description: When you first boot up the game you are greeted with the best intro of a vr game ever! I’m not joking it’s something you’d never expect but at the same time it’s SO Twilight Zone. Good job devs!
The hame definitely has an amazing story telling aspect. I wish there was more. It is is pretty addicting and fun. Hopefully they release multiple seasons of the game!

developer_response.body:
Hi rockymonk1 - 
Thanks for the great review! Glad to hear that you enjoyed your time in, The Twilight Zone.

reviews.review_description: By far one of my favorites so far..very fun experience 💯

I love the twilight zone. The original and the new one, and the game lived up to the shows. Very cool. I just wish it had been longer. On the first segment it took me a while before I realized the voice was mine! It was creepy & very twilight zoney! Haha.

Its a rather immersive game that really gets your heart pumping. There's great graphics and an even better story line but be warned some aspects are legit frightening. My apple watch kept telling me I needed to breath. I have only bought a handful of games ~10 since owning my headset but this is an easy favorite for me. I highly recommend giving it a go!!!!!!

developer_response.body: So glad to hear that you enjoyed your time in The Twilight Zone! Thank you for the kind words.

reviews.review_description:
I had so much fun with this game.
Beautiful, immersive. Play this game!

developer_response.body: Many thanks for journeying to The Twilight Zone

reviews.review_description: I’m going nowhere; it’s way too scary. Besides, my only real defense is some soda cans for distraction. I don’t see myself making it to the elevator since I can’t find the card.
